events.label: assistant
events.text: Diagnosis written to `/app/answer.json`. Summary: the missing load reactor on long VFD motor leads creates an impedance mismatch that reflects the drive's fast switching edges back down the cable, causing reflected-wave voltage transients (potentially 2x+ the drive output) with very high dV/dt at the motor terminals. This progressively stresses phase/turn insulation, especially on non-inverter-duty motors, risking premature winding failure — a non-compliant condition per manufacturer cable-length limits and NEC 430 motor-circuit provisions. I flagged it as `fail` / `high` risk with corrective actions (verify lead length vs. mfr max, install a properly sized reactor/filter, megger/surge-test the motor, consider interim carrier-frequency reduction).
